hero.label: SRHR · HIV/AIDS Awareness · Community Empowerment
hero.title: Creating Awareness, Reducing Stigma & Empowering Communities
hero.subtitle: RAPP is a Kigali-based NGO working with urban and rural communities to educate youth on Sexual and Reproductive Health, prevent HIV/AIDS through theater, and support income-generating activities for vulnerable people.

features.title: Theater & Awareness
features.desc: Using theater, songs, poetry, and creative media to spread messages about HIV/AIDS prevention and Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights.

features.title: Health Outreach
features.desc: Community theater troupes reach thousands with messages on HIV prevention, family planning, and reducing stigma against people living with HIV.

features.title: Income Generating Activities
features.desc: Supporting vulnerable and low-income people — especially those infected or affected by HIV — through business cooperatives and self-help groups.

features.title: Community Partnerships
features.desc: Collaborating with government, national and international NGOs to implement donor-funded projects across urban and rural Rwanda.

missionSection.label: Who We Are
missionSection.title: Awareness, Empowerment & Dignity for Every Rwandan
missionSection.body: RAPP was founded in 2003 in Kigali as a local NGO implementing projects from international donors. We work across urban and rural districts, recognizing the link between HIV prevention, stigma-reduction, and taboos around sexual relations. Through theater and creative outreach, we educate young people on SRHR and HIV/AIDS — while also reducing poverty by supporting income-generating cooperatives run by local self-help groups.
